description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: archipelagic country in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: The Philippines, officially the Republic of the Philippines, is an archipelagic country in Southeast Asia.

state of the United States of America
description_wiki: South Dakota ( ; Siouan languages: Dakȟóta itókaga, pronounced [daˈkˣota iˈtokaga]) is a landlocked state in the Upper Midwest, North Central region of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Nebraska (  nə-BRASS-kə) is a triple-landlocked state in the Midwestern region of the United States.

description: part of the United Kingdom situated on the island of Ireland
description_wiki: Northern Ireland is a part of the United Kingdom in the north-east of the island of Ireland.

description: Cunard Line ocean liner, also used as a merchant cruiser and troop transport
description_wiki: RMS Aquitania was a British ocean liner operated by the Cunard Line from 1914 to 1950.

aliases: Stormont Castle Stormont Estate Upper Newtownards Road Belfast
description: mansion in Belfast, Belfast district, Northern Ireland, UK
description_wiki: Stormont Castle is a manor house on the Stormont Estate in east Belfast which is home to the Northern Ireland Executive and the Executive Office.

description: oldest golf course in the world
description_wiki: The Old Course at St Andrews, also known as the Old Lady or the Grand Old Lady, is considered the oldest golf course in the world.

description: 1943 military campaign of World War II on the island of Sicily, Italy
description_wiki: The Allied invasion of Sicily, also known as the Battle of Sicily and Operation Husky, was a major campaign of World War II in which Allied forces invaded the Italian island of Sicily in July 1943 and took it from the Axis forces defended by the Italian 6th Army and the German XIV Panzer Corps.

description: 1944 landing operations of the Allied invasion of Normandy
description_wiki: The Normandy landings were the landing operations and associated airborne operations on 6 June 1944 of the Allied invasion of Normandy in Operation Overlord during the Second World War.

description: United States Army general (1885–1945)
description_wiki: George Smith Patton Jr.

description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: United States Armed Forces decoration award
description_wiki: The Bronze Star Medal (BSM) is a United States Armed Forces decoration awarded to members of the United States Armed Forces for either heroic achievement, heroic service, meritorious achievement, or meritorious service in a combat zone.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: country in western Europe
description_wiki: Belgium, officially the Kingdom of Belgium, is a country in Northwestern Europe.

description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

description: low mountain range in Belgium, France, Luxembourg and Germany
description_wiki: The Ardennes, also known as the Ardennes Forest or Forest of Ardennes, is a region of extensive forests, rough terrain, rolling hills and ridges primarily in Belgium and Luxembourg, extending into Germany and France.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: capital and most populous city in France
description_wiki: Paris is the capital and largest city of France, with an estimated city population of 2.04 million in an area of 105.4 km2 (40.7 sq mi), and a metropolitan population of 13.2 million as of January 2026.

description: former commune in Manche, France
description_wiki: La Haye-du-Puits (French pronunciation: [la ɛ dy pɥi]) is a former commune in the Manche department in Normandy in north-western France.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: island in New York, United States of America
description_wiki: Long Island (abbreviated as LINY) is a densely populated continental island in southeastern New York state, extending into the Atlantic Ocean.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Texas (  TEK-səss) is the most populous state in the Southern United States.

description: anti-aircraft warfare
description_wiki: The 8.8 cm Flak 18/36/37/41 is a German 88 mm anti-aircraft and anti-tank artillery gun, developed in the 1930s.

description: military base in San Antonio, Texas
description_wiki: Fort Sam Houston is a U.S. Army post in San Antonio, Texas.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: city and county seat in Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Pittsburgh ( PITS-burg) is a city in Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, United States, and its county seat.

aliases: Colonia Claudia Ara Agrippinensium - in Laitn
description: most populous city in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany
description_wiki: Cologne (  kə-LOHN; German: Köln [kœln] ; Kölsch: Kölle [ˈkœ̂lə] ) is the fourth-most populous city of Germany and the largest city of the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia with nearly 1.1 million inhabitants in the city proper and over 3.1 million people in the Cologne Bonn urban region.

description: city in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany
description_wiki: Aachen is the 13th-largest city in North Rhine-Westphalia and the 27th-largest city of Germany, with around 261,000 inhabitants.

description: organization of U.S. war veterans
description_wiki: The American Legion, commonly known as the Legion, is an organization of U.S. veterans headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana.

aliases: Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States
Veterans of Foreign Wars of the U.S.
description: organization of U.S. war veterans
description_wiki: The Veterans of Foreign Wars (VFW), formally the Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States, is an organization of United States war veterans who fought in wars, campaigns, and expeditions on foreign land, waters, or airspace as military service members.

description: veterans’ organization
description_wiki: The Disabled American Veterans (DAV) is an organization created in 1920 by World War I veterans for disabled military veterans of the United States Armed Forces that helps them and their families through various means.

description: non-profit organizations that transport US military vets to see the memorials of the respective war(s) they fought
municipal public golf course owned by the city of San Diego, California
description_wiki: Torrey Pines Golf Course is a 36-hole municipal golf facility in University City, a community of San Diego, California.

description: neighborhood in San Diego, California, United States
US federal government agency that maintains information on those potentially subject to military conscription
description_wiki: The Selective Service System (SSS) is an independent agency of the United States government that maintains a database of registered male U.S. citizens and other U.S. residents potentially subject to military conscription (i.e., the draft).

description: military boarding school in Millersburg, Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Millersburg Military Institute (MMI) was a military boarding school founded in 1893 in Millersburg, Kentucky, about 30 miles (48 km) northeast of Lexington, Kentucky.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Kentucky (US:  , UK: ), officially the Commonwealth of Kentucky, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.
